items.title: C&E prototype — AWC portfolio family-disambiguation rating
Preserve 2025 EPR NOW — check Workday Canceled section; download PDF + screenshot My Reviews if present; if gone, screenshot the absence (dated)
Monday (priority): issue the authorized FTO search orders for the AWC NPI programs — get searches started to deliver
Compile IP-deliverable status summaries for AWC June + July NPI gate reviews (per IP Deliverables deck) - reply to Sandra
Send Sandra the AWC NPI FTO scope + cost + budget-alignment email (decision-forcing; DRAFT in body for review)
Request Power Automate loop/iteration permission (C&E 315-file batch)
Obtain updated Flagship IP/status spreadsheet (from Shannon) — current status + patent numbers
Puffin / NPWT FTO-complexity presentation — pitch broadly (IP + broader Legal + business) using Sandra's "how did we miss this" as the hook
Prep + script for 6/26 1:1 with new AWC president Kelley Moffett (present NPWT IP deck; corroborate with consultant digital-maturity assessment)
P4P / Workday Compensation Statement — preserve + track salary-increase discrepancy (1.5% shown vs recollection of a different figure)
Draft 2026 Individual Development Plan (IDP) in Workday — due June 30; discuss at July 1:1
Decide whether/when to follow up with Anthony Tropiano (R&D sponsor) - HOLD until after the 6/26 Kelley 1:1; value-additive framing, not role-positioning
Counsel consult — pin down EEOC filing deadline(s) for ADA demotion claim
Monitor AWC IP lead req posting (daily check)
Gate review prep: find next date, secure attendance (Anthony or self), review prior slides + pre-read, generate value-demonstrating questions
Schedule Anthony + Neelu strategic follow-up (settlement strategy)
Prepare IP-focused questions on Leah vs Copilot for legal presentation
Update master employment memo (source of truth)
Organize ADA / HR / Sedgwick records
Prepare IP/business questions for R&D on Acera with NPWT
Meet with Neelu (IP continuity outreach)
Meet with Jake (IP continuity outreach)
Create master list of active work workstreams
Follow up with Sandra (after Anthony)
Reach out to Anthony (IP continuity)
Make work plan for lead IP position
Prep Jake meeting deliverables (Smith & Nephew NPWT + Brad's narrow pattern coverage)
NPWT portfolio enhancement project — execution plan
Identify follow-up action items from Brent presentation — map each to senior leader + format
Reply to Scott's April review — brief human reply
Forward all four Scott monthly reviews + April review email to personal Gmail
Pull all prior Scott written praise — forward to personal email
Save Sandra's "Scott's reports report to me" email — forward to personal + PDF
Monthly performance update to Scott
Schedule follow-up meetings with senior leaders on Brent presentation action items — by May 1 EOD
Pull HR records confirming formal title (Sr. Managing IP Counsel) vs role description (patent specialist)
Begin scope narrowing — operate within JG15-defined responsibilities only
Schedule employment lawyer consultation — BEFORE May 16, 2026

Photo the 2026 Workday Goals deck (3 slides: Top Quartile Performer / Trusted Partner / Best Place To Work) AND the AWC IP Division of Responsibilities Excel (capture BOTH tabs — Sheet1(1) and Sheet1(2) visible). Personal phone, private cloud folder. Foundational smoking-gun evidence — the two strongest pieces in the entire file.
Thursday morning reset — read new Sandra emails fresh, reassess Lisa reply strategy, then draft both responses

Draft Halunen re-engagement email (Charles Horowitz + Jackie Olson). Now writable with materially stronger fact pattern: Scott departed May 1, lead role open again, Workday Goals describe lead-counsel scope, Division of Responsibilities co-authored with Scott, Sandra April 2/May 5 self-contradiction on WHT settlement, May 4 written charter, May 5 'recommendations to IP attorneys' subordination language.
Reply to Lisa Fulton's AWC IP Priorities email (May 4 1:12 PM) — scope clarification questions. Mom surgery was Tue 5/5. Pushed to Fri 5/8 to allow Thu morning reset.

URGENT — Re-engage Halunen Law (Charles Horowitz) per his Dec 19 2025 roadmap. Conditions for revisiting (company response + future status change) have materialized. Send brief update email referencing severance offer, role-restoration denial, written charter, Scott departure, and litigation CC exclusion. Also reach out to Darren Sharp at Schaefer Halleen (untried referral; reinstatement-focused, hourly basis).
Research → decide whether to apply for ManyVids CEO AI-pivot role

Prep for Tuesday 5/12 12:30-1:00 PM ET 1:1 with Sandra (interim manager) — NOTE: meeting confirmed via calendar invite as Tuesday May 12 (NOT Monday May 11) and only 30 minutes. Review notes-taking approach, prepare 'rating project status' update, prepare neutral req-timing question, draft post-meeting written follow-up template. 30 min is tight — plan for 2-3 substantive items max.
Resolve TX TCHRA / TWC 180-day pathway question with counsel BEFORE Sat May 16 2026 (Nov 17 2025 act clock)
Decide filing strategy with lawyer — file now vs. wait for hiring decision
Pick representative NPWT family for AWC Copilot agent test run
Plan R01129974 application strategy — what to write, what to omit, internal-audience framing
Update resume for R01129974 application (per strategy plan)
5/11 Mon — First 1:1 with Sandra as interim manager
Sandra 1:1 — 5/12/2026 detailed response playbook (scripts, recap email template, HR email template)
R01129974 - Résumé Build & Application (working doc)
6/9 Sandra 1:1 - agenda & prep
Session handoff - 6/11/26 EOD: Sandra purview email + Friday 6/12 double-meeting setup
Friday 6/12 playbook: NPI decision menu + IPR (Meditech) meeting + Sandra 6/11 reply
Puffin / S&N FTO complexity + 'why we missed the reference' learnings deck - plan (Sandra-assigned 6/12)
Session handoff: 6/9/26 EOD - work/deliverables track
Work project handoff prompt — paste as new Work-project custom instructions (case-file routing, calibration discipline, current state, reconciliation flags)
HEIC emails processing — delegation prompt for separate chat (14 unprocessed Outlook email screenshots)
Session handoff — 5/8/26 EOD state (entry point for fresh chats)
Reference: M365 Copilot — what's safe, what isn't
Prompt: EP Opposition tracker — consistency check
Prompt: Monthly Sandra Report — Excel analysis (stage 1)
10% process-time reduction (May 4 charter)
UPC strategy (May 4 charter)
Prompt: Email recall and triage
Prompt: Monthly Sandra Report — Word draft (stage 2)
Prompt: Outside counsel routine instruction email
Prompt: Critical reader pre-send review
Prompt: Pre-meeting context pull
Strategy: Sustainable-load operating mode (year-or-two horizon)
Recurring: AWC EP opposition tracker maintenance
PROJECT: May 4 2026 Sandra Charter — execution & scope clarification
Prompt: Invention disclosure intake brief
Recurring: Monthly portfolio actions + 2026 savings + forward savings (Sandra)
2025 EPR rated 'Successful' — AUTHORED by Sandra (managed Suraj most of the review period), DELIVERED by Scott 3/5/2026; positive narrative ('leadership in assessing options,' 'growth mindset')
Protected activity #2: Suraj's Feb 10 2026 formal reconsideration request to Michael Morimoto (HR), citing the Robergelund admission; HR responds there is 'no reconsideration option'
2025 year-end review found in Workday's 'Canceled' section (discovered 5/8/2026), breaking 5 consecutive years (2021-2024) of 'Effective performance' ratings
Denial rationale #1 (Sedgwick examiner): early-return request characterized as an 'inappropriate request'
Reusable Prompt - Apply Work OS Goal-First Principles Globally (Claude Code / Codex)
Cluster 1 — Silence Inventory (6/19 Strategic Posture Reconsideration session)
FTO Budget — AWC line items detail + Concur search quotes (6/19; confirmed from Liz + Concur call; updates 382c50314bb6814380ade3af2de0459e)
Agent Briefing Packet - Work/ADA (Codex/agent-ready, full incl. evidence inventory) - 6/18
Strategic Posture Reconsideration (6/18) - from silence to record; + Claude Code prompt for the deeper interactive pass
FTO + proactivity defense - CONSOLIDATED strategy & framings to hold (6/18 synthesis; start here)
6/18 IP meeting: Andrea publicly modeled the proactive NPI-tracker standard; Suraj raised PMO gate-schedule/PDT-slides - adopt the tracker as inoculation + lead-ownership evidence
Proactive-FTO process vs. role structure (IP Counsel directs FTO; Specialist does groundwork) - non-proactivity-criticism risk and the scope-vs-tier read
FTO info comes from dev teams; prosecution + portfolio strategy needs business context (stage-gate/business-review materials) - basis for a separate access ask
Scott held the AWC NPI FTO and did not assign it to Suraj; gap surfaced on Sandra's NPI-FTO-status inquiry (Suraj recollection — to corroborate)

HYPOTHESIS (claimant-hat, speculative) — prior Anthony Tropiano (R&D sponsor) contact (~early May 2026) may have prompted Sandra's 5/4 scope-narrowing charter (AA2); unverified, temporal proximity noted, for counsel
EVIDENCE — Kelsey MacConaghy 1:1 (~6/17/26): Andrea Evensen shares post-PDT (gate-review) info with her IPSS Patent Specialist; comparator to Sandra not sharing the equivalent with Suraj (AWC)
EVIDENCE — Sandra 6/17/26 Teams thread: AWC NPI FTO budget + vendor cost (Cardinal ~$5K/search vs Concur), offset rules, 'budget part of your goals'; Sandra defers the search decision to Suraj
Sandra 6/11/26 looped Suraj into the Meditech IPR as a required attendee (outside-counsel strategy call, Fri 6/12) - litigation-inclusion data point

Sandra 6/11/26 9:52 AM email to Lisa Fulton + Nicole Linden (cc Suraj): 'Suraj will review the portfolio... in his new role handling all of AWC other than Acera' - third-party-directed scope statement
Sandra 6/11/26 written reply: purview = ALL patent and FTO work for ASC + AWC (only Acera excluded); directs AWC strategy authorship; IPR = Meditech, Friday meeting
6/9 1:1: Sandra reviewed 2026 Workday Goals (first time); NPWT CI deck send is goal-execution follow-through, not manager request - corrects earlier characterization
Sandra 5/8/26 written directive: internal signature to show Patent Specialist role instead of title (Roland + Kelsey also named); external signature may keep title

Resume calibration principles (6/10/26) - mirror the reader's criterion language; strengthen via content specificity not verb strength; claim work and event, never adoption or impact, when reception was weak; temper absolutes to bounded claims; avoid ambiguous category words like 'strategy'

⚖️ CONSTRUCTIVE DISCHARGE + SEPARATION ECONOMICS (6/10/2026) — never quit as a litigation tactic: a resignation that fails constructive discharge cuts off back/front pay at the resignation date and makes the case LESS fundable; the CD bar (Suders) is high and current facts very likely don't meet it; the separation upside (~5-10x wage engine) comes only from Solventum's acts (R01129974 rejection, post-charge retaliation/termination) or a counsel-negotiated exit

🔁 FRESH-PASS RECONSIDERATION (6/10/2026) — standing decisions hold; revisions: R01129974 now the single most urgent item (the sent letter promises 'I plan to apply shortly' in writing); contingency downgraded to plausible-but-unproven; hard backstop = paid hourly consult by ~6/30 (earliest plausible federal clock ~Sept 13 2026 per Brief §5A, verified this session); FCHR dual-filing preserves the FCRA uncapped lever; firm follow-ups ~6/16-17 (phone for Castronovo + Hadsell Stormer)

Litigation involvement - extraction-vs-development tension + WHD business-reporting exclusion (Ender's recollection: not included on Sandra's reporting to the business on a matter he was supposed to be on). Resolution: softened 'glad to get more involved' line in the 6/10 recap (involvement, not task absorption); scope-shaping is behavioral (full IPR engagement = compounding work; meter if technical-only dumping; document exclusions); WHD final-report probe as separate learning-framed ask with dual value either way
R01129974 résumé — defensibility framework + honest-framing standard (applied 6/9/2026)
LLMs do judgment; code does orchestration (AI-tool architecture)

💰 FORWARD-LOOKING DAMAGES MODEL (6/9/2026) — three-layer theory (back pay + front pay/lost earning capacity + emotional distress) on the claimant's actual inputs (age 42; base ~$260k preserved; ~13% bonus; ~$30k equity; LTI $60k→$40k); two but-for scenarios (no-promotion vs Senior Director); FCRA uncaps compensatory (ADA caps comp+punitive at $300k); stacked best-case ceiling ~$2.5–4.5M incl. fees vs realistic high-6-to-low-7-figure settlement; age-42 + capacity double-edge on front-pay duration

⚖️ DON'T-DO + EEOC procedure (6/9/2026) — do NOT pre-disclose the draft EEOC charge to Solventum GC/litigation counsel (no leverage benefit, slight leverage cost); an EEOC charge + right-to-sue is a prerequisite to an ADA district-court suit; pro se is fine to preserve the charge but advised against for the litigation itself (expert-driven front-pay theory; contingency representation likely available)

ADA claim characterization - failure-to-restore (reopened role) and an R01129974 rejection read PRIMARILY as RETALIATION, pleaded alongside discrimination. Why retaliation fits this act (post-complaint timeline; unassailable qualifications/pretext; reopening guts the 'role eliminated' defense; independent of the discrimination merits). Discrimination still leads at AA1. Causation-standard split (Nassar but-for vs. motivating-factor) is contested/jurisdiction-specific, counsel to pin.

AWC IP strategy deck - provenance & reception: SOLICITED by Brent (interim AWC president) as an IP position update; presented to the business leadership team (VPs Neelu + Jake + SVP R&D Anthony attended); Brent stepped out / didn't really see it; SVP R&D engaged & positive; the two VPs' follow-up meetings were CANCELED (hypotheses open: transition churn most likely vs. weak impression vs. Sandra interference; re-engagement is the right move regardless)

Scope-contradiction-as-evidence + the credit-theft worry's real remedy is PROVENANCE, not dilution. Corrects the overstatement that IP strategy is 'squarely expected' of the Patent Specialist role - it is contested OUT of the role by the May 4 'support and track' charter (AA2), which is precisely why Sandra assigning Suraj strategy work BUILDS his case. Produce requested work competently + attributed; don't volunteer extra; don't water down.

6/9/26 Sandra 1:1 (2nd monthly) - EPR re-entry SECURED (Sandra agreed to fix); FTO/NPI-gate + GD-SB deliverables NOT addressed (still open); NEW asks: Karola budget-tool review, send strategy slides, write up UP/UPC strategy, fresh end-to-end Anaqua process proposal; litigation: Suraj asked to be more involved, Sandra to forward at least the IPR; Anja to stop validation/precommittee; info-flow exclusions noted but not raised; role chart + attorney-status HELD per plan

Attorney-status argument already disclosed to HR (Feb 10 2026 reconsideration) - so the cost of raising it with Sandra in a 1:1 is FORUM + contemporaneous-record, NOT secrecy; preserve the role-scope contradiction for counsel/EEOC, do not deploy in the 1:1. PLUS Ender's hypothesis that the Feb 10 protected activity may have hardened the non-attorney boxing (test against the timeline).

⚖️ CASE-LAW RESEARCH (6/7/26) — 11th Cir./FL ADA & FCRA, leave or accommodation → adverse-action comparators + temporal-proximity doctrine + emotional-distress & career-harm damages (figures, §1981a caps, FCRA uncapped-comp lever) + both-side litigation positions. ILLUSTRATIVE not controlling; confirm all citations against Westlaw/Lexis before any filing reliance.
📝 ATTORNEY INTAKE WRITEUP (final, bulleted) — reusable claimant-hat outreach text for firm intake forms / calls. Use for O&G, Nichols Kaster, Schwartz (after conflicts), Sass consult.

⚖️ ATTORNEY OUTREACH STATUS — 6/9/2026: SENT to 4 national front-pay firms (HMRH · Castronovo · Hadsell Stormer · BGL), awaiting replies; clear conflicts (HMRH, Castronovo) before sharing detail. Front-pay/lost-earning-capacity lens governs fit. Shortlist, verified contacts, conflicts-opener decision + FINAL letter in body. Pipeline (O&G best-documented fit, etc.) in the 5/29 record below.

⚖️ MN FORUM / MHRA FORECLOSED (5/29/26) — employer's MN HQ insufficient; user never resided/worked in MN. Controlling: Kuklenski v. Medtronic, 8th Cir. 4/9/2025. Operative law = federal ADA + FL FCRA (+ possible TX). Retracts prior 'MN strategic advantage' flag.
Counsel outreach screener — SENT 5/29/2026 (ADA matter; strongest-evidence-first)

⚖️ ATTORNEY LIST 2nd RE-CALIBRATION (5/27/26 evening) — USER IS IN FL — SUPERSEDES first re-calibration 36ec50314bb68144abbfe92542218f5c. New FL-resident tier-1 lead: SASS, EVERHART & SILVA (Tampa, 813-251-5599) — Lawdragon, FL Bar Board Certified, NELA Florida Chapter leadership, explicit ADA practice, hourly consults. SHSM Nashville and Outten & Golden remain tier-1 national alternatives (work remotely). Nashville-as-local angle from prior Insight is retracted.

⚖️ ATTORNEY LIST RE-CALIBRATED (5/27/26 evening) — SUPERSEDES first-pass list 36ec50314bb681b382dbd8ec6b9d80af. New tier-1 lead: SANFORD HEISLER SHARP McKNIGHT (Nashville office, LOCAL to user, 615-442-6886). Tier-1 alternatives: Outten & Golden (NYC, national reach), Rogge Dunn Group (Dallas TX, board-certified-in-two). Pollard PLLC and other first-pass candidates demoted to second-tier only.

⚖️ ATTORNEY EXPANSION — 5 new FL/TX executive-focused plaintiff candidates (5/27/26). Pollard PLLC (Fort Lauderdale), Derek Smith Law Group (Miami), Jason L. Gunter (Fort Myers); Rogge Dunn Group (Dallas), Shellist Lazarz Slobin (Houston). Supplements the Game Plan 5/26 lawyer-path list.

🎯 STRATEGIC FRAMING — case goals hierarchy, leverage architecture, 5-ramp exit model, R01129974 interview scenarios (5/27/26). Complements the 5/26 Game Plan Insight with the deeper strategic backbone the Game Plan operates on tactically.
C&E project priority: BUILD the system rapidly = #1; executive deck/story is SECONDARY, reverse-engineered post-build from real design rationale

Kelsey 1:1 (5/27/26) — Andrea framed Kelsey as a partner; Kelsey reported difficulty distinguishing Patent Specialist from Lead IP Counsel roles; Suraj told Kelsey for Sandra the role is supposed to be more patent prosecution focused. Direct comparator data point: IPSS Lead↔Specialist framing (Andrea partnership) differs from AWC framing (Sandra's May 4 'support and track' charter). Suraj characterized the relationship with Kelsey as one where he can be 'pretty frank and honest' and she appreciates it.

STRATEGIC GAME PLAN (5/26/26, current operating strategy) — (1) WORK POSTURE: competent floor on everything assigned/documented, NOT minimal (underperformance hands Solventum a clean non-discriminatory reason for adverse action and wrecks both the ADA claim and severance leverage); discretionary excellence only where it compounds (case file / CV / outside-silo relationships / leverage). (2) R01129974: APPLY — REVERSES the earlier 'do not apply' recommendation in Insight 361c50314bb6812391fad829da2192a0; apply ~June 1-3 as a serious candidate, expect rejection, value the record. (3) EEOC CLOCK: ~300-day deadline likely ~early-Oct 2026 (demotion enacted Dec 11 2025), BEFORE the planned Nov reassessment — counsel consult within 2-4 weeks to pin the exact deadline. (4) Refines the Medela Insight framing. Supersedes earlier conflicting advice as noted.

PATTERN (running list) — Things Suraj previously requested/advocated and was denied or deferred, now treated by Sandra/Solventum as default or new-leadership initiative. Establishes the pre-restructure constraints were CHOICES, not necessities (company's current willingness to resource these things shows they were always resource-able). Instances: (1) outside counsel support for Medela patent enforcement — requested, not approved; Sandra now defaults to third-party involvement; (2) direct reports / managing attorneys — requested pre-leave, deferred by Sandra; restructure created the manager-managed relationship, given to Scott; 'management of attorneys' now an R01129974 requirement. DISCIPLINED FRAMING: facts only, no characterization of WHY (animus vs. ordinary corporate transformation is for counsel). Add instances as they surface.

EVIDENCE + STRATEGIC — Sandra 5/21/26 'FW: Patent Enforcement - Medela' delegation. Sandra forwarded Suraj's own Aug 2022 Medela presuit-enforcement email + asked him to identify patents to assert and send patent numbers; she will have a third party check infringement evidence. THRESHOLD ISSUES Suraj identified (which Sandra's 'keep it moving' framing skips): strongest candidate patent expired 2023 (no injunctive leverage, closing past-damages window); claim-construction lock-in constrains future flexibility vs Smith & Nephew (the bigger competitor); Medela is #2 US tNPWT but ~5-8% share, modest. Recommended reply: answer + surface threshold considerations as IP counsel informing a decision, not resistance. Attorney-tier IP litigation work delegated under Patent Specialist designation (same pattern as Defcon, R01129974 specs). 2022 email documents Suraj's pre-demotion title 'Senior Intellectual Property Counsel.' Sandra Outlook flag due 5/25/26. COMPETING PRIORITY: C&E prototype end-of-May.

ACTION + EVIDENCE — Sandra 5/21/26 'FW: Open IS's - No Final Disposition' delegation. Sandra forwarded Liz Johnson's open-invention-disclosure report; asked Suraj to disposition pre-2026 AWC open IDs (pending since 2022, 2024, 2025). QUICK WIN - reconciles with backlog-cleanup work Suraj already doing with Anya (inventor follow-ups to close old IDs). AWC has 23 open IDs in the report (2+ from 2022, 3+ from 2024). Genuine patent-specialist-tier operational work (contrast: Medela is attorney-tier). Liz/Nicole-IP-Ops separately reworking invention submission process (inventor-selected Business Chargeable, Submitted to Final Disposition flow, auto Awaiting Pre-ranking) targeting June completion - overlaps Anaqua workstream. Sandra Outlook flag due 5/25/26. Lower priority than C&E prototype (end-of-May).

EVIDENCE — 5/15/26 Defcon Biohacking proposal escalation. Sandra delegated narrow IP-spotting task; Suraj escalated SAME EVENING to senior MedSurg business-law leadership (Ajit Narang, GC-side) with substantive cross-functional risk analysis framing it as broader Legal/Regulatory matter. Attorney-tier judgment (governance, stakeholder mapping, deference on ownership, competitive intelligence citing Medtronic/BD/Stryker/Cardinal) delivered under Option 2 'Role: Patent Specialist' signature. Same-day parallel: Sandra's 5/15 R01129974 specs response (Insight 361c50314bb6812391fad829da2192a0) listed required capabilities for the externally-recruited Assistant Chief IP Counsel role — every one of those capabilities except 'management of attorneys' (the circular requirement) was demonstrated by Suraj in the Defcon escalation the same day. Continues pattern from April 29 IP training delivery (35fc50314bb6813781ecd86372f29b2f) of attorney-tier work preserved organically under demoted role-designation.

EVIDENCE + STRATEGIC ANALYSIS — Sandra's 5/15/26 10:20 AM R01129974 role specs response (full verbatim preserved). Sandra cites 'management of attorneys (strongly preferred)' as key requirement — but CIRCULAR: pre-leave NO Lead IP Counsel had direct reports; Suraj asked for them, Sandra deferred; same restructure that demoted Suraj CREATED the management responsibility and gave it to Scott; Suraj was THE FIRST Patent Specialist (prototype). Other specs (competitive intelligence, strategic insight, IP litigation, IP attorney standards) MATCH Suraj's Scott-approved 2026 goals. Only genuine differentiator is M&A. 'Those skills [patent prep/pros] are less in use on a day-to-day basis' = Sandra's written confirmation the role oversees the work Suraj now does. Sandra chose to put detailed elevated specs in writing in response to Suraj's inquiry — the specs are now on paper at her hand. Strengthens pretext theory incrementally; clean structural argument against 'role evolved beyond Suraj's capabilities' defense; modestly improves severance-leverage scenarios; doesn't transform case but materially adds to existing strong pretext layer.

FOUNDATIONAL — Solventum IP Org Structure (authoritative reference, 5/15/26). PRE-LEAVE: 5 Lead IP Counsels (one per business), all flat-reporting to Sandra, NO direct reports. 'Lead IP Counsel' = the role; titles by JG (15=Sr Managing IP Counsel; 16=Director & Asst Chief IP Counsel — Suraj's pre-leave; 17=Sr Director & Asst Chief IP Counsel). RESTRUCTURE: announced Nov 17 2025, enacted Dec 14 2025 on Suraj's return. NEW STRUCTURE: each Lead IP Counsel gets one Patent Specialist where business has enough patent work — 3/5 have separate Patent Specialists (Dental/Roland, AWC/Suraj, IPSS/Kelsey 5/18); 2/5 don't (HIS/Bender, Drinking Water/Bramwell). SURAJ WAS THE FIRST PATENT SPECIALIST — structure went live with his return as the prototype. READ THIS INSIGHT FIRST for any future org-structure question.

EVIDENCE — ONE Nov 3 2025 LinkedIn posting for 'Assistant Chief IP Counsel, Director*' (Suraj's formal pre-leave title) at $259-316K. Posting title was generic across the 5 businesses, but AWC was the only open such seat at posting time — effectively recruiting Suraj's role. Posted ~9 days before Suraj's Nov 12 ADA request; taken down shortly after; predates HR's Nov 14 job-protection threshold. Per Suraj 5/15: Sandra never informed the IP team; Scott saw it on LinkedIn himself and was annoyed Sandra wasn't sharing info. Andrea (joined IPSS Lead Jan 2026) was the candidate hired through this same single posting, redirected into Scott's vacated IPSS seat via the internal cascade (Scott moved to AWC Dec 9, 2 days before Suraj's Dec 11 return) — Suraj's speculation but well-grounded. R01129974 (May 2026 for AWC Lead) was announced internally via Sandra's team email — the anomaly vs. the Nov 3 secrecy practice. Full Nov 3 evidentiary record (Memo's Exhibit O, held strategically) at Insight 358c50314bb681529ad6ecda40dfe6cb.
